text: Day 33 night (8:20 PM PDT): Trump address — 2-3 weeks, April 6 deadline; Netanyahu 'Ten Plagues'; Iran denies ceasefire; Brent $106/WTI $104; Vafaei + Hajj Sadiq killed; 46 Hezbollah waves; IDF 400+ strikes in 48h; Isfahan bunker-busters; Kuwait airport hit; gas $4.06; UK Hormuz conf tomorrow.
verificationNote: Compiled from CNBC, AAA, Ynet, Al Jazeera, Times of Israel, Alma Center.

text: IRGC just threatened 18 major US tech companies (Apple, Google, Meta, Nvidia, Tesla, Boeing) — ordered employee evacuations. Markets rattled. This is unprecedented: first time a state actor has directly threatened named corporations as military targets.
verificationNote: CNBC and Time confirmed IRGC statement.

text: 4 Iranian missile salvos overnight — cluster warheads again. 2 babies among 4 wounded in Bnei Brak. Meanwhile Hezbollah launched 50+ rockets at northern Israel on Passover morning. Coordinated timing continues: Iran + Hezbollah striking simultaneously.
verificationNote: Times of Israel liveblog and IDF confirmed.

text: Oil +8% this morning — Brent $109.16, WTI $108.36. Complete reversal of yesterday's peace-hope dip to $101. Trump's 'hit extremely hard' vow + April 6 Hormuz deadline = markets pricing in escalation, not peace. Goldman now sees $110 average through April.
verificationNote: CNBC confirmed price data, Goldman forecast per EIA STEO.

text: UK 35-nation Hormuz conference starting now — US conspicuously absent. FM Cooper chairing. France, Germany, Italy, Canada, UAE, Japan all present. This could be the beginning of a non-US Hormuz reopening framework. Military planning session to follow.
verificationNote: Al Jazeera and Asia Times confirmed summit.

text: China-Pakistan 5-point peace plan is the first credible ceasefire framework from a major power. Key: ceasefire + Hormuz reopening packaged together. Pakistan serving as US-Iran mediator. Axios says US may not oppose behind scenes. Watch for Iran's response.
verificationNote: Middle East Eye, Axios, SCMP confirmed plan details.

text: Day 34 AM (4:40 AM PDT): Brent $109/WTI $108 (+8%); 4 Iran salvos overnight (cluster, 4 hurt); Hezbollah 50+ rockets; IRGC threatens 18 tech giants; UK 35-nation Hormuz summit; China-Pakistan 5-pt peace plan; Rubio-Hegseth ground troop feud; Argentina IRGC designation; gas $4.064.
verificationNote: Compiled from CNBC, Times of Israel, AAA, Al Jazeera, Middle East Eye.

text: Pasteur Institute of Iran struck by US-Israeli airstrike — century-old medical research center (founded 1920) destroyed. Iran Health Ministry: 'barbaric assault on international health security.' 316 healthcare centers now damaged since Feb 28. WHO documented 24+ attacks on health infrastructure.
verificationNote: Common Dreams, Al Jazeera liveblog, Middle East Eye confirmed.

text: US Embassy Baghdad just issued urgent alert: Iranian-backed militia attacks expected in central Baghdad within 24-48 hours. All US citizens told to leave immediately. Airspace closed. This is the strongest Baghdad warning since the war began.
verificationNote: Official US Embassy security alert confirmed by Al Arabiya, Business Today.

text: 40-nation Hormuz conference just concluded (expanded from 35). US notably absent. Coalition signed statement demanding Iran reopen strait. Iran-Oman separately finalizing draft Hormuz navigation regime — first diplomatic framework for post-war transit. Markets partially recovering on the Oman news.
verificationNote: Al Jazeera, CNBC, Washington Times confirmed conference outcome and Iran-Oman draft.

text: Mobarakeh Steel Complex in Isfahan now completely shut down — recovery estimated 6-12 months. Iran's largest steel producer. Khuzestan Steel in Ahvaz also offline. Iran calls it 'economic terrorism.' Meanwhile Guterres warns world is 'on edge of wider war.'
verificationNote: Al Arabiya confirmed via company official. UN News confirmed Guterres statement.

text: Day 34 PM (10:33 AM PDT): Brent $109/WTI $111 (volatile +8-13%); gas $4.08; Pasteur Institute struck; Baghdad militia warning; 40-nation Hormuz summit (US absent); Iran-Oman navigation draft; Guterres 'edge of wider war'; 2 IDF killed in Lebanon; S&P -0.81%; gold -2.7%.
verificationNote: Compiled from CNBC, Common Dreams, Al Jazeera, AAA, UN News, Yahoo Finance.

text: Hegseth just fired Army Chief of Staff Gen. Randy George — effective immediately. Replaced by his own military aide Gen. LaNeve. Major wartime leadership upheaval. Bipartisan criticism already pouring in. This is the first firing of an Army Chief during active combat operations in modern US history.
verificationNote: Breaking Defense, Washington Post, NPR confirmed.

text: Evening Iranian salvo: ~30 missiles fired at Israel, most intercepted. One hit Petah Tikva industrial zone — cluster bomblets scattered to nearby playground. 11-year-old girl critically wounded. This is the third documented cluster munition attack on populated areas this week alone.
verificationNote: Times of Israel confirmed with images. HRW documented cluster munition use.

text: Houthi ballistic missile targeting Tel Aviv area intercepted. Separately, 50+ Hezbollah rockets fired at northern Israel — 2 lightly wounded. Triple-axis coordination continues: Iran + Hezbollah + Houthis all striking in the same evening window.
verificationNote: Times of Israel liveblog confirmed all three attack vectors.

text: Macron says military Hormuz reopening 'unrealistic' — contradicts UK-led push. Calls for ceasefire first. Criticized Trump: 'When you want to be serious, you don't say the opposite every day.' France breaking from UK on Hormuz military options.
verificationNote: Bloomberg and France24 confirmed Macron statements.

text: Markets staged remarkable intraday reversal: Dow was -600 pts but closed just -61 (-0.13%). S&P actually closed +0.11%. Recovery driven by Iran-Oman Hormuz navigation regime draft news — first diplomatic framework for post-war transit. Oil still at $109/$111.
verificationNote: CNBC and Motley Fool confirmed market close data and recovery narrative.

text: Day 34 EVE (3:58 PM PDT): Army Chief fired; Petah Tikva cluster strike (girl critical); Houthi + Hezbollah evening attacks; Macron 'unrealistic' Hormuz military; markets recovered (S&P +0.11%); Brent $109.35/WTI $111; gas $4.08; 40-nation Hormuz summit; April 6 deadline in 4 days.
verificationNote: Compiled from Breaking Defense, Times of Israel, Bloomberg, CNBC, AAA.
